title: invAIriant self-audit #2 — the CLI, skill, and audit-memory build (phases 1–6)
scope: The subsystem built across phases 1–6: `invairiant collect`, the audit-pr UX, `render-comment`, the `validate-report` semantic linter, committed audit memory (`record`/`history`), and the skill wiring. Applied to the framework's own repo. Out of scope: the lens library and docs prose (covered by self-audit #1).
inputs_reviewed: git log 5d1ee7b..HEAD (6 phased commits, all CI-green)

lens_scores.verdict: the CLI/skill boundary holds: the CLI runs no lenses and produces no findings; judgment lives only in the skill/prompts
the semantic linter encodes the verdict↔severity and reference-integrity invariants; `record` is now idempotent by audit label
six small phased commits, CI green throughout, docs/CHANGELOG synced; only gap is unit tests vs CI smokes (CLOSE-003)
one story held across six phases — skill primary, CLI seatbelt, memory sanitized; no entity sprawl
the CLI stayed narrow — ten deterministic subcommands, no creep toward 'auditing'
error messages are actionable, but audit memory resolves relative to CWD (CLOSE-001) and the collect fallback scans all files (CLOSE-002)
record sanitizes and stores no raw evidence; raw bundles gitignored; subprocess list-form (no shell); no secrets committed

findings.claim: Audit memory is resolved relative to the current working directory, so running the CLI from a subdirectory silently misses the committed .invairiant/history/.

evidence.description: the validate-report memory hook opens Path('.invairiant/history/rejected-hypotheses.jsonl') relative to CWD
record/history default --dir '.invairiant/history' is CWD-relative, not repo-root-relative

findings.risk: A subdirectory run reports 'no known_rejected' and skips the memory-aware warning with no error — a silent false negative, not a failure.
findings.recommendation: Resolve the history dir from `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` (or a config key) with a CWD fallback.

findings.claim: The `collect` grep fallback (no ripgrep) iterates every tracked file until the per-category cap is hit, so on a very large repo with few matches it scans the whole tree.

evidence.description: _grep fallback loops over all `git ls-files` entries reading each line-by-line; bounded on output (cap) but not on files scanned

findings.risk: Slow bundle build / high I/O on large repos; acceptable for the per-PR target use but unbounded in the worst case.
findings.recommendation: Cap files/bytes scanned in the fallback, or prefer ripgrep and warn when absent on a large tree.

findings.claim: The CLI is covered by CI smokes only; there is no unit-test suite for the semantic linter, record idempotency, or render logic.

evidence.description: no tests/ directory; .github/workflows/validate.yml exercises happy paths and one negative case, but there are no unit tests pinning _semantic_report_errors, cmd_record dedup, or render-comment output

findings.risk: Regressions in edge cases are caught only if a smoke happens to exercise them.
findings.recommendation: Add a small pytest suite for the semantic linter rules, record idempotency, and render-comment formatting.

hypotheses.text: `invairiant record` duplicates memory rows when run twice on the same report, skewing history trends.
hypotheses.rejected_reason: Refuted at HEAD — record now dedups by audit label (cli/invairiant.py:790-799); re-running record on the self-audit prints 'already in memory — skipping' and leaves the CSV at 6 rows. Fixed in this phase; kept here as the record of the decision.

hypotheses.text: The local authorship hook could block legitimate commits.
hypotheses.rejected_reason: Refuted — the commit-msg hook rejects only Co-Authored-By/Assisted-By trailers naming an AI agent; ordinary commits (no such trailer) pass. Verified across six phased commits, all accepted.

summary.executive_summary: The six-phase build holds together: the CLI stayed a narrow, judgment-free seatbelt (parnas 9, dijkstra 8), the semantic linter and now-idempotent record encode real invariants (cormen 8), and secrets/raw-evidence handling is sound (security-threat 8). No S0/S1. Three low-severity items remain: audit memory is CWD-relative (CLOSE-001, S3), the collect fallback is unbounded in files scanned (CLOSE-002, NOTE), and the CLI has CI smokes but no unit tests (CLOSE-003, NOTE). The record-duplication risk raised during the audit was fixed in-phase and kept as a recorded, refuted hypothesis.
summary.weakest_reason: audit memory resolves relative to CWD (CLOSE-001) and the collect fallback scans all files (CLOSE-002) — both operability papercuts, neither blocking

required_actions.action: Resolve .invairiant/history/ from the repo root, not CWD.
Add a pytest suite for the linter, record idempotency, and render logic.

reviewer_notes: Second audit recorded to .invairiant/history/, giving `invairiant history` a real per-lens trend. Scope is the phase-1–6 subsystem, scored on the same core lenses as self-audit #1.
